visual_attributes.morphology: ground-level cloud composed of tiny ice crystals
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: crystals are tiny, layer depth varies from 20 to 300 meters

domain_specific_visual.OPTICAL_EFFECTS: associated with halos, sun dogs, and light pillars
domain_specific_visual.FORMATION_CONDITIONS: forms under clear or nearly clear skies

functional_attributes.primary_function: reflects and refracts light, creating optical phenomena
secondary_functions: contributes to precipitation in polar regions

domain_specific_functional.WEATHER_INDICATOR: indicates temperature inversion and high relative humidity
domain_specific_functional.VISIBILITY_EFFECT: may reduce visibility to under 600 meters

relationships.description: Most frequently observed in the interior of Antarctica
Commonly observed in the Arctic regions

contextual_attributes.historical_period: observed throughout history in cold climates
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Antarctica, Arctic, and any cold region
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbolizes extreme cold and pristine natural beauty
